page_title: Termination law and at-will exceptions — Mississippi
html_title: Mississippi Termination Law and At-Will Exceptions 2026 | Teamed
meta_description: Mississippi at-will employment, the narrowest public-policy exception in the US (McArn crime-refusal rule only), no state final-pay statute, and the federal claim layer every employer still faces.

hero.alt: Mississippi State Capitol building in Jackson, classical dome and portico facade under a clear morning sky, flanked by mature oak trees.
hero.credit: Illustration: Mississippi State Capitol, Jackson · Teamed editorial

key_legal_facts.public_policy_exception: McArn only — refusal to commit illegal act. 626 So. 2d 603 (Miss. 1993).

provisional_facts: McArn citation — 626 So. 2d 603 (Miss. 1993). Verify reporter citation.
180-day EEOC window — confirm no FEPA worksharing agreement as of 2026.
Miss. Code § 71-1-35 — confirm 50-employee twice-monthly pay frequency threshold is current.
Federal-only WARN — confirm no Mississippi state WARN analogue passed since cache date.
Miss. Code Ann. § 25-9-171 — confirm state-employee-only scope as of 2026.

flags.description: Five statutory citations marked provisional per phase-1.0 spec. Verify before publish: McArn reporter cite, 180-day EEOC window (no worksharing), § 71-1-35 pay-frequency threshold, federal-WARN-only status, § 25-9-171 private-sector scope.
